business.headline: Sensex falls 1,110 points, Nifty below 24,500; Tata Steel tumbles 7%The BSE Sensex fell 1,122.66 points, or 1.40%, to close at 79,116.19, while the Nifty 50 declined 385.20 points to settle at 24,480.50.

Tata Steel, L&T, JSW Steel: Why these stocks fell up to 8% todayShares of Tata Steel, Larsen & Toubro and JSW Steel plunged sharply as global tensions and rising crude oil prices rattled markets. Read on to understand what triggered the sell-off today.

Strait of Hormuz shutdown: Is India staring at an energy crisis?Iran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil route. Read on to understand what it could mean for India's energy supplies and economy.

Rupee slumps to all-time low against US dollar: Why is it falling?The rupee came under pressure as escalating tensions between the US, Israel and Iran triggered global risk aversion, while rising crude oil prices and a stronger dollar weighed on emerging market currencies.

L&T shares tumble over 7%: Why is the stock down today?L&T shares dropped nearly 7% during the session as investors turned cautious amid escalating tensions involving the United States, Israel and Iran.

Sensex down 1,500 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Dalal Street opened sharply lower on Wednesday as escalating geopolitical tensions and rising crude prices rattled global markets. Here are the key reasons behind today's sharp stock market fall.

Panic grips Dalal Street as war tensions sink Sensex by ovr 1,700 pointsDalal Street investors turned cautious as crude oil prices surged and fears grew that a prolonged war in West Asia could disrupt global energy supplies and stoke inflation.

Is the stock market open or closed on Holi, March 4?Wondering if you can trade stocks on Holi this year? While celebrations continue across India on March 4, here's what you need to know about NSE and BSE trading.

India has enough oil stocks, no immediate plan to hike fuel prices: Govt sourcesIndia has 25 days of crude oil and refined oil stocks and is scouting for alternative sources for importing crude oil, LPG and LNG, government sources have confirmed. There is no immediate plan to raise the price of petrol and diesel, according to government sources. However, there is bound to be impact where gas and oil is concerned with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. About 80 per cent of the oil that India depends on comes from Iran and through the Strait of Hormuz. The Strait of Hormuz has been shut for the past 48 hours. Energy analyst Chetan Bhutani states that 'strategic reserves can support LPG and LNG demand for about 15 to 20 hot days, if needed, while crude oil reserves are estimated to last about 40 to 45 days in the event of supply disturbances.'

23-year-old turns India's instant house help craze into $100 million startupFrom that single hub in Gurugram to a presence across 10 cities, the growth has been swift. Today, nearly half of Pronto's demand comes from the New Delhi region, with about a fifth from its home base of Bengaluru. The platform now clocks more than 18,000 bookings daily, and over 4,500 professionals worked at least one shift on the app last month.

Iran–US conflict puts India's basmati exports at risk, farmers fear lossesIndia sends nearly 72% of its total basmati exports to Middle Eastern countries. As conflict spreads in the region, uncertainty has started to cloud this important trade. If the war continues for a long time, exports could fall sharply, and farmers may end up paying the price.

India has enough oil stocks for 50 days, looking for more options: Govt sourcesGlobal oil prices rose for the third straight session on Tuesday. Traders are worried that supplies from the Middle East, one of the world's most important oil-producing regions, could be disrupted.

I have Rs 50,000. Should I open a shop or invest in financial assets?Rs 50,000 may not look like a big amount, but it can be the starting point of something meaningful. It could turn into a small business you build with your own hands, or quietly grow through smart investments. The choice you make today could shape your financial future.

Dubai caught in geopolitical storm. Will it hurt its billionaire-friendly image?Dubai has thrived by being the Gulf's safe address and has long promised stability in a turbulent region. Now, with conflict edging closer, that promise is being quietly tested.

India trims gas supply to industries after Qatar halts LNG production: ReportQatar halted its LNG production on Monday as Iran continued strikes in the Gulf in response to Israeli and US attacks. The situation has disrupted energy shipments and pushed up global fuel prices and shipping costs.

Gold jumps nearly Rs 5,000: Will prices continue to rise amid the US-Iran war?The yellow metal is shining once again as geopolitical uncertainty fuels fresh buying interest. As the US-Iran war unsettles global markets, investors are wondering, will gold continue its upward climb from here?

Why is stock market closed today when people are celebrating Holi on March 4?The Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE) have declared March 3 as the official trading holiday for Holi 2026 in their annual calendar.

EPFO interest stays at 8.25%: Why has the rate not increased this year?At a time when households are watching every rupee, even a small rise in EPF returns would have felt welcome. Instead, the rate remains at 8.25%. Here's why the retirement body has chosen stability over a hike.

Oil climbs for third day as US-Israel conflict with Iran threatens supplyThe main concern in the market remains the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway through which about 20% of the world's oil and gas supply passes.

Holi 2026: Is stock market open or closed today?The stock market is closed on March 3, on account of Holi 2026. While the exchanges have declared the holiday for Tuesday, the festival of colours will be observed across many parts of the country on Wednesday, March 4.

entertainment.headline: Stressed out and nervous: Lara Dutta stranded in Dubai with daughter Lara Dutta on Wednesday shared she is stuck in Dubai with her daughter Saira. She praised the UAE government for ensuring the safety of citizens.

Rashmika-Vijay Hyderabad reception: Allu Arjun, Ram Charan-Upasana arrive at venue Allu Arjun, Ram Charan and Upasana Konidela were among the attendees at Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda's reception in Hyderabad.

Kriti Sanon, Karan Johar and Homi Adajania arrive at Rashmika-Vijay's reception Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda greeted the photographers at their invitation-only wedding reception in Hyderabad. Kriti Sanon, Karan Johar and Homi Adajania also attended the event.

Made In Korea trailer: Priyanka Mohan heads from Tamil Nadu to Seoul The trailer for Made in Korea, released on Holi, introduces viewers to Shenba's emotional journey as she navigates cultural boundaries between India and South Korea. The film, starring Priyanka Mohan, is set to premiere on March 12.

Vijay finally breaks silence amid Jana Nayagan delay. Check out what he said Actor and Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) chief Vijay addressed supporters in Thanjavur on Wednesday. He spoke of the delay in release of his film Jana Nayagan, and was critical of the DMK government and Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin.

Is Cillian Murphy playing Voldemort in new Harry Potter series? Here's what he said Cillian Murphy spoke about reports of his casting as Lord Voldemort in the new Harry Potter series. He also addressed speculation about his return in the next film of the 28 Years Later franchise.

Aamir Khan backs Leonardo DiCaprio, says theatrical business needs protection Aamir Khan backed Leonardo DiCaprio's view on protecting theatrical run of films, saying the current balance between theatres and streaming platforms is not a "graceful compromise."

Jake Gyllenhaal on sister Maggie: She's directed me since childhood Actor Jake Gyllenhaal shared what it was really like being directed by sister Maggie in The Bride!, saying she has "been directing" him since childhood.

Viral pic: Ranbir Kapoor, Aryan Khan, Varun Dhawan at a friend's birthday party Ranbir Kapoor, Aryan Khan, Varun Dhawan, Ahan Shetty and Arjun Kapoor were clicked at a private birthday party. Their picture has gone viral and has sparked speculation: Is there a collaboration waiting to happen?

K.Jo, Kriti Sanon, Shahid Kapoor, more: Rashmika-Vijay's reception guest list Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda's Hyderabad reception will see a star-studded guest list that includes celebrities from the Telugu film industry and Bollywood.

Boong trailer: Young boy's journey of hope amid conflict anchors BAFTA winner The Manipuri-language film Boong, written and directed by Lakshmipriya Devi, prepares for its theatrical release on March 6, 2026, following a historic BAFTA award win. The film follows a young boy's heartfelt adventure to reunite his family.

Yash's Toxic trailer launch cancelled after film release shifted to June The trailer launch for Yash's Toxic: A Fairytale for Grown Ups has been cancelled as the film's release date moves from March to June 2026. The change follows cinema disruptions in the Middle East.

True Lies stars Arnold Schwarzenegger, Jamie Lee Curtis in California Hall of Fame Arnold Schwarzenegger and Jamie Lee Curtis are set to join the 19th California Hall Of Fame. The accolade honours "trailblazers whose achievements embody California's spirit of resilience and creativity."
Ananya Panday shares glimpse of Holi celebrations with her parents and pets Ananya Panday celebrated Holi with parents and pets. The actor shared a glimpse of the family festivities on social media.

Chikni Chameli is a hit but Shreya Ghoshal won't sing such a song again. Here's why Shreya Ghoshal addressed backlash over performing Chikni Chameli while choosing not to record similar songs today. The singer said she owns the hit but is now more mindful about what is now perceived as lyrics and objectification.

Actor Elnaaz Norouzi on Iran: Like India, it was diverse and not solely Islamic Iranian-German actor Elnaaz Norouzi compared Iran's past to India's diversity and spoke of democracy, identity and fear of returning home. Her remarks have sparked fresh debate.

Vijay Crishna, SRK's Devdas co-actor, dies at 81; Anupam Kher pays tribute Shah Rukh Khan's Devdas co-actor Vijay Crishna died at the age of 81. Anupam Kher, Lillete Dubey and others paid emotional tributes to the late actor.

The Kerala Story 2 producer Vipul Shah slams Sudipto Sen criticism: Grapes are sour The Kerala Story 2 has stirred fresh controversy with producer Vipul Amrutlal Shah firmly rejecting criticisms about the film's research and certification. Despite disputes and legal questions, the sequel has successfully hit Indian theatres.

Rajpal Yadav denies crying in court, calls social media reports imaginary Actor Rajpal Yadav dismissed reports claiming he cried in court or said he had no money, calling them "imaginary stories". The actor was arrested and later released on bail in a 2012 cheque-bounce case.

general news.headline: 87 killed as Iran warship hit by US sub near Sri Lanka, was returning from IndiaThe United States submarine sank an Iranian warship off the southern coast of Sri Lanka, US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth confirmed on Wednesday.

Nitish Kumar's Rajya Sabha nomination today, Bihar may get new BJP CMThe move hints at a potential change in the state's political future. Speculations are high that the BJP might opt for the Chief Minister's post once Kumar gets elected to the Rajya Sabha.

Russia says ready to ship 9.5 million oil barrels to India amid supply crisisIndia has witnessed a vulnerability in terms of its crude reserves, following the breakout of the Middle East crisis. At present, it has crude stocks covering 25 days of demand along with low inventories of gasoil, petrol, and liquefied petroleum.

Eden haunts South Africa again as Finn Allen storms Kiwis into T20 World Cup finalUnbeaten till the semi-final, South Africa were knocked out of the T20 World Cup by New Zealand. The Aiden Markram side failed with both bat and ball and ended up losing at Eden Gardens by 9 wickets.

In Mysore Silk saree and ivory veshti, Rashmika-Vijay go traditional at receptionRashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda's reception look was minimal yet brimming with subtle traditional and cultural details.

Live: Trump proves he 'does not bluff' with Iran strikes, White House saysUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The conflict between Iran and the US-Israel alliance continues to intensify on Wednesday, the fifth day of direct military confrontation, with no immediate signs of de-escalation. At least 787 people have been killed in Iran, nearly 60 in Lebanon, around a dozen in Israel, and six US soldiers have also died. Israel launched fresh strikes on Iran and renewed attacks on Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, including airstrikes in and around Beirut. Iran has retaliated with counterstrikes on Iran and US interests in the Gulf. Follow IndiaToday.in for live updates on the escalating Middle East conflict.

Congress gets 28 assembly constituencies as seat-sharing deal with DMK finalisedBesides the 28 assembly constituencies, the party also got one Rajya Sabha berth as part of the Secular Progressive Alliance.

US sinks Iran warship in India's strategic waters. Why this is significantAmerican forces torpedoed Iranian warship IRIS Dena in the Indian Ocean, near Sri Lanka. It was returning from Visakhapatnam after a multilateral naval exercise. The sinking of an Iranian ship in India's strategic waters but outside its maritime jurisdiction is significant. This is our analysis of the situation.

Stressed out and nervous: Lara Dutta stranded in Dubai with daughterLara Dutta on Wednesday shared she is stuck in Dubai with her daughter Saira. She praised the UAE government for ensuring the safety of citizens.

In a first, Iran and Hezbollah launch coordinated missile strikes on Israel: ReportThe Israeli military said a barrage of missiles was launched from Iran while several projectiles, including drones, were fired by Hezbollah from Lebanon at roughly the same time. The attacks triggered red alert sirens across major Israeli cities, including Jerusalem and Tel Aviv, sending civilians rushing to shelters.

Apple launches budget MacBook Neo, its most affordable laptop ever, starting at Rs 69,900It's official, Apple has launched the most affordable MacBook ever. It is called the MacBook Neo and here is everything you need to know about it.

No money or medicine: Telugu tourists' SOS to PM Modi from BahrainA group of tourists from Telangana and Andhra Pradesh stranded in Bahrain are facing a dire situation amid missile and bomb incidents at the airport.

Rashmika-Vijay Hyderabad reception: Allu Arjun, Ram Charan-Upasana arrive at venueAllu Arjun, Ram Charan and Upasana Konidela were among the attendees at Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda's reception in Hyderabad.

How Robotic Knee Replacement is Redefining the Journey Back to MobilityEarly consultation with an orthopaedic specialist is essential. Delaying surgery in severe cases can lead to worsening deformity and reduced functional outcomes.

My WhatsApp chats can't prove cruelty: Court cancels divorce granted to husbandThe Bombay High Court has set a precedent by overturning a divorce decree granted solely on WhatsApp evidence, emphasising the need for both parties to be heard.

Pakistan signals it may be drawn into Iran war. Here's whyIshaq Dar, Pakistan's foreign minister, said he warned his Iranian counterpart against attacking Saudi Arabia and cited the defence pact. This is the first time that any Pakistani official has clarified that the pact would apply to the Iran war.

Is Cillian Murphy playing Voldemort in new Harry Potter series? Here's what he saidCillian Murphy spoke about reports of his casting as Lord Voldemort in the new Harry Potter series. He also addressed speculation about his return in the next film of the 28 Years Later franchise.

Arvind Kejriwal's 'Kattar Imandar Holi' jibe at BJP after Delhi liquor scam reliefThe former Delhi Chief Minister coined the term 'Kattar Imandar' after being discharged in the Delhi liquor scam case last week. He also accused the BJP of hatching a political controversy against the Aam Aadmi Party.

Trump fights Trump as US strikes IranHandcrafted ByOur Editors As the US launched its most serious and fraught military operation in decades, President Donald Trump has delivered anything but clarity on his justification for the Iran war, his goals and the timeframe. Trump and his team now find themselves entangled in a self-contradictory mess. We explain how.

Touched by God: The many shades of Jasprit BumrahIndia vs England: Jasprit Bumrah has not been used in the T20 World Cup as a traditional spearhead of the bowling attack. India Today delves into how Bumrah has adapted to this new role and where his skills stem from.

How did the Strait of Hormuz get its name? Here's the real origin storyThe Strait of Hormuz is one of the world's most strategic waterways, but where did its name come from? Is it Persian, Zoroastrian, or something else entirely? Here's the layered history behind the name that has echoed across trade routes for centuries.

Sharad Pawar named Opposition MVA's pick for lone Rajya Sabha seat in MaharashtraThe decision came after all the stakeholders, the NCP(SP), Congress, and Shiv Sena (UBT), unanimously supported the four-time Chief Minister's name for the Rajya Sabha.

Is there a Chinese secret behind Iran's deadly missile and drone arsenal?Iran's massive firepower has been on display in the last couple of days. China delivered weapons to Iran right ahead of the US-Israeli strikes, reported the Middle East Eye. Does the huge missile and drone arsenal that Iran is using have a Chinese link? This is what we can tell you for sure.

Video: Moment US submarine struck Iranian warship 'IRIS Dena' near Sri LankaThe video, shared by the US Department of War, showed unclassified drone footage of the Iran's IRIS Dena moments during the strike. The ship initially appears intact and moving steadily across calm waters. A sudden, massive explosion then erupts near the stern, sending up a huge plume of white water, fire and debris.

sports.headline: India are the favourites, but T20 is a fickle game: Harry Brook before semis T20 World Cup 2026, IND vs ENG: Speaking to reporters, England skipper Harry Brook acknowledged that India would start as favourites in the semi-final at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai, especially with home support behind them. However, he also said that T20 cricket is an unpredictable format where momentum can shift rapidly within a matter of overs.

Aiden Markram laments poor batting approach after semi-final exit vs New Zealand South Africa captain Aiden Markram blamed his team's batting approach after their semi-final loss to New Zealand. Finn Allen's record century sealed New Zealand's spot in the T20 World Cup final.

Finn Allen's 33-ball hundred in numbers: How special was his Eden blitz T20 World Cup, SA vs NZ: Finn Allen broke quite a few records with his century as New Zealand raced to the final after beating South Africa by nine wickets at the Eden Gardens in Kolkata.

Both you fossils: Dinesh Karthik's unmissable banter with Hussain, Atheron Former India cricketer Dinesh Karthik has responded strongly to criticism from former England captains over India's Champions Trophy 2025 victory. He highlighted Pakistan's failure to capitalise on similar advantages at the T20 World Cup 2026, pointing to a clear skill gap.

Eden haunts South Africa again as Finn Allen storms Kiwis into T20 World Cup final Unbeaten till the semi-final, South Africa were knocked out of the T20 World Cup by New Zealand. The Aiden Markram side failed with both bat and ball and ended up losing at Eden Gardens by 9 wickets.

Some things never change: South Africa crumble under semifinal pressure once again SA vs NZ, T20 World Cup 2026 semifinals: South Africa's familiar batting frailties resurfaced at the worst possible time as fans on X tore into the Proteas following another crushing collapse in the T20 World Cup 2026 semifinal in Kolkata.

Daniil Medvedev, Andrey Rublev to miss Indian Wells exhibition amid Middle East tensions Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have disrupted international tennis, forcing Daniil Medvedev and Andrey Rublev to miss the Eisenhower Cup at Indian Wells due to Gulf airspace closures.
Sanju Samson made sure he was always ready for India: Morne Morkel Here's the full transcript of Morne Morkel's press conference before India vs England semifinal in Mumbai.

Satwik-Chirag suffer shocking first-round exit in All England Open All England Open 2026: Satwik and Chirag's campaign came to an abrupt end as the Indian duo were stunned by Malaysia's Aaron Tai and Khai Xing Kang in a disappointing first-round exit

England put their faith on data, video analysis in semi-final against India IND vs ENG semi final: England will be relying on data in their T20 World Cup semi-final against India, said Harry Brook. England are set for a steep test against the hosts at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai on Thursday, March 5.

Off-field chaos, on-field heartbreak: India women fall to Vietnam in Asia Cup opener India's AFC Women's Asia Cup campaign began in heartbreak as they lost 2-1 to Vietnam in Perth, days after a jersey fiasco saw the AIFF send ill-fitting kits to the squad ahead of the tournament.

An honours board for sports journalists: Mumbai celebrates cricket's storytellersIn a rare and thoughtful gesture, the Mumbai Cricket Association unveiled a Journalists' Honours Board at Wankhede Stadium on Wednesday, recognising the writers and broadcasters who have spent decades chronicling cricket's triumphs and memories.

SA vs NZ Highlights, semifinal: Finn Allen's 100 powers New Zealand into finalSouth Africa (SA) vs New Zealand (NZ) Highlights, T20 World Cup: Finn Allen scored an unbeaten 100 as New Zealand beat South Africa by 9 wickets at the Eden Gardens in Kolkata and booked their berth in the final.

Samson jumps 25 places in ICC T20I rankings after 97-not-out vs West IndiesIndia opener Sanju Samson has jumped 25 places in the ICC men's T20I batting rankings on Wednesday, 4 March. The boost in the ranking comes right after Sanju's tremendous show against West Indies.

Pakistan drop Babar Azam for Bangladesh ODI tour after poor T20 World CupPakistan Cricket Board has dropped star batter Babar Azam from the upcoming ODI tour of Bangladesh. This move signals a shift towards fresh talent following Babar's recent poor form in international cricket.

Abhishek Sharma's No.1 rank in danger as Pakistan's Farhan gets closer to top spotT20 World Cup: Abhishek Sharma retained his No. 1 position in the ICC T20I batter rankings, but Pakistan's Sahibzada Farhan has closed in on the top spot following an impressive run in the tournament where he scored 383 runs.

Is India's fielding woes down to mindset? Morkel dodges bouncer before IND v ENGT20 World Cup: Morne Morkel opened up about India's fielding woes at the T20 World Cup 2026, ahead of their semi-final clash against England in Mumbai.

Dube can still give us couple of overs: India bowling coach MorkelT20 World Cup: India bowling coach Morne Morkel has backed Shivam Dube despite his expensive spells in the T20 World Cup. Morkel said the all-rounder still has the ability to provide India with a couple of useful overs.

Kagiso Rabada backed to step up in semifinals despite poor luck in T20 World Cup 2026SA vs NZ, T20 World Cup 2026 semifinal: South Temba Bavuma has backed Kagiso Rabada to deliver in the T20 World Cup semifinal against New Zealand, insisting the pacer's hard luck with dropped catches has masked his true form.

A man possessed: Inside Hardik Pandya's power-hitting marathon in MumbaiT20 World Cup: As India fine-tuned preparations for the semifinal against England at the Wankhede Stadium, batters came and went through the nets. Hardik Pandya stayed. For nearly three hours, he kept swinging, turning the training session into a night of relentless power-hitting.

No retirement yet: Glenn Maxwell wants to continue playing T20Is for AustraliaT20 World Cup: Australia's Glenn Maxwell has said that he isn't ready to call time on his career just yet, aiming to make it to the LA Olympics and the next edition of the T20 World Cup in 2028.

Hardik Pandya's aura and ego: Broad sounds alarm on India's big threat ahead of semisIND vs ENG, T20 World Cup 2026 semifinal: Stuart Broad has warned that Hardik Pandya's aura and self-belief make him a massive threat in the T20 World Cup semifinal, cautioning that England could be in serious trouble at the death.

Former captain Sarfaraz Ahmed set to be named as new Pakistan Test team coachSarfaraz Ahmed is expected to take over as Pakistan's Test head coach for the upcoming series against Bangladesh, with his appointment reportedly expected to be confirmed in the coming days.

T20 World Cup 2026 breaks viewership record, becomes most-watched editionT20 World Cup: The ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 has become the most-watched edition ever, crossing 500 million viewers in India. ICC chairman Jay Shah confirmed the milestone as the tournament heads into the semi-final stage.

Tennis star Aryna Sabalenka announces engagement with partner Georgios FrangulisWorld No.1 Aryna Sabalenka announced her engagement to boyfriend Georgios Frangulis on Wednesday, March 4. Sabalenka is gearing up for the French Open next after reaching the final of the Australian Open.

Podcaster opens up on viral Stuart MacGill crash out episode: He tried to bully meAndrew Menczel has responded after his heated on-air clash with Stuart MacGill went viral. The podcaster said the former Australian spinner's behaviour crossed the line and felt like an attempt to bully him.

Sanju Samson fandom on par with Dhoni, Kohli and Rohit craze: Dinesh KarthikT20 World Cup: Dinesh Karthik has claimed that Sanju Samson has a similar fandom to MS Dhoni, Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli in India. Samson's knock against the West Indies helped India qualify for the semi-finals of the T20 World Cup 2026.

Pakistan team to be fined? Amir slams Naqvi, PCB for making players scapegoatsT20 World Cup: Mohammad Amir slammed Mohsin Naqvi and the Pakistan Cricket Board for reportedly slapping the players with hefty fines after their poor performance in the T20 World Cup 2026.

Rodrygo heartbroken after ACL injury spoils World Cup dream: Do I deserve this?Rodrygo will miss the rest of Real Madrid's season and the upcoming World Cup after suffering an ACL and meniscus tear. The Brazilian forward called it one of the worst days of his life.

Arjun Tendulkar's sangeet: Yuvraj, Harbhajan add to cricketing royalty at eventArjun Tendulkar and Saaniya Chandhok's wedding festivities began in Mumbai on March 4, following pre-wedding celebrations in Jamnagar. Yuvraj Singh, Harbhajan Singh and others brought a touch of cricketing royalty to the event.

MS Dhoni fined for overspeeding, traffic violation in RanchiMS Dhoni has been fined Rs 1000 after his vehicle was caught overspeeding in Ranchi. The e-challan was issued under Section 183 of the Motor Vehicles Act ahead of IPL 2026 preparations.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
